metrics.title: Record Backlog
metrics.label: +22% QoQ, +79% YoY (Q1 2026); data centre + mining led

moat.description: The world's largest construction and mining equipment manufacturer with the deepest global dealer network, a growing autonomous mining fleet (800+ trucks), and structural exposure to AI data centre power demand — durable franchise with cyclical overlay.
moat.analysisTitle: The Dealer Network + Autonomous Mining + Data Centre Power Moat
moat.analysisSummary: Caterpillar's moat is **the deepest global dealer network in heavy equipment paired with autonomous mining technology leadership and a structural AI data centre power tailwind** — durable across cycles:

analysisPoints.title: Unmatched Dealer Network Density
analysisPoints.text: Caterpillar's ~160 independent dealers operate ~2,700 branch locations worldwide — the largest service network in heavy equipment. Dealer proximity means faster parts delivery, lower downtime, and higher machine utilisation. Mining and construction customers value uptime above all else; switching to Komatsu or Volvo means accepting weaker service coverage in most geographies. This creates a self-reinforcing flywheel: more machines in a region attract more dealer investment, which attracts more buyers.

analysisPoints.title: Autonomous Mining Technology Leadership
analysisPoints.text: Caterpillar's Command autonomous haulage system operates 800+ trucks globally, having moved 11+ billion tonnes without a lost-time injury. Vale is quintupling its autonomous CAT fleet to ~90 units by 2028. At CONEXPO 2026, CAT showcased autonomous excavators for construction, and in 2026 CAT began developing autonomous retrofit kits for third-party trucks (including Komatsu's 930E) — a signal the Command platform is becoming an industry standard rather than a CAT-iron-only feature. The autonomous installed base creates a data flywheel — more autonomous hours generate better algorithms, which attract more mining customers to the platform.

analysisPoints.title: Energy & Transportation — Data Centre Power Tailwind
analysisPoints.text: CAT's Energy & Transportation segment (~41% of revenue) supplies reciprocating generators, gas turbines, and power systems for data centres. AI infrastructure buildout is driving structural demand growth — Q1 2026 E&T revenue surged, and management raised the 5-year forecast on power and energy. This is a long-duration tailwind tied to global AI capex, not a one-quarter phenomenon.

analysisPoints.title: Services Flywheel — 40% of Revenue, Growing
analysisPoints.text: About 40% of Caterpillar's sales now come from recurring, higher-margin services — parts, rebuilds, Cat Financial, and digital fleet management (VisionLink, Cat Connect). Equipment life cycles of 15-25 years generate decades of aftermarket revenue per unit sold. Services revenue grew to a record $24B+ in 2024-2025, with margins structurally higher than new equipment.

growth.description: Q1 2026 revenue of $17.4B (+22% YoY) beat consensus by $0.9B; adjusted EPS $5.54 (+30% YoY). FY2025 delivered record $67.6B revenue and $9.5B ME&T free cash flow. Management raised FY2026 guidance and the 5-year outlook, driven by data centre power demand and mining autonomy. Tariff headwind of $2.2-2.4B in FY2026 partially offset by pricing and mix.

growthAnalysis.scoreDerivation: Base 73 (8-12% CAGR mid-band) + 1 trajectory (E&T accelerating) + 4 margin expansion (services + data centre mix shift) + 4 both (data centre TAM + mining autonomy) - 5 cyclicality risk (tariffs + macro sensitivity) = 77

drivers.name: Energy & Transportation
drivers.metric: Data centre power + gas turbines; +22% Q1 2026 revenue; 5-year forecast raised

drivers.name: Resource Industries
drivers.metric: Mining capex recovery + autonomy; 800+ autonomous trucks; Vale fleet 5× expansion

drivers.name: Construction Industries
drivers.metric: Infrastructure spending (IIJA); non-residential construction; modest volume growth

growthAnalysis.keyRisk: If AI data centre capex moderates below 15% growth in 2027 simultaneously with a mining capex downturn, E&T and RI growth halves and the premium multiple compresses from ~40× to 22-25× — CAT already trades at a three-decade-high price/sales ratio, and Michael Burry's July 2026 short disclosure shows sophisticated capital is already positioning for that de-rating. CAT has historically experienced 30-40% drawdowns on cyclical scares.

valuation.description: At ~$955 CAT trades 5% above the base case ($910) and roughly 16% of the way toward the bull case ($1,200) — the AI-infrastructure narrative has pushed price/sales to its highest level in at least three decades. The stock hit an all-time high of ~$1,065 on June 30, 2026 before pulling back after Michael Burry disclosed a short position on July 1 citing overvaluation. Record backlog and the data centre tailwind support elevated demand, but margin of safety is thin and re-rating risk is now visibly in play.

peAnalysis.summary: CAT trades at 40× forward earnings vs. a typical industrial peer median of 22-25× — a premium that has widened further as Wall Street treats CAT as a backdoor AI-infrastructure play (shares roughly doubled over the trailing year). PEG of 3.3× and a 30-year-high price/sales ratio leave little room for execution slips. Michael Burry's July 2026 short disclosure — his first-ever bet against the name — is a visible signal that sophisticated investors view the current multiple as stretched, even though the underlying backlog and guidance remain strong.

bear.description: Cyclical downturn plus tariff escalation compresses earnings and re-rates the multiple from 38× to 25× on depressed EPS.
points: AI data centre capex decelerates below 10% growth in 2027; E&T power revenue plateaus
Mining capex cycle turns down on commodity price weakness; autonomous fleet expansion slows
Tariff costs exceed $2.5B annually with no pricing offset; operating margin compresses below 16%

base.description: FY2026 EPS lands at consensus ~$23, FY2027 reaches ~$26 as data centre and mining tailwinds sustain, multiple holds at 35×.
points: E&T continues 15%+ growth on AI data centre buildout and energy transition
Autonomous mining fleet expands to 1,000+ trucks by end of 2027
Services revenue grows to 42-45% of total mix, supporting margin expansion

bull.description: AI data centre super-cycle drives E&T to new highs, autonomous mining scales globally, FY2027 EPS exceeds $30 and multiple expands to 40×.
points: AI infrastructure capex sustains 25%+ growth through 2028; CAT becomes a recognized AI-infrastructure beneficiary
Autonomous mining achieves commercial deployment at 5+ new major mining customers by end of 2027
Construction autonomy (autonomous excavators) reaches early commercial deployment, opening a new TAM

learnedInterfaces.note: N/A — heavy equipment manufacturer; operator familiarity exists but is not a software interface moat.

businessLogic.note: VisionLink, Cat Connect, and autonomous fleet management software encode customer-specific workflows. Growing but secondary to the hardware franchise — less embedded than Deere's Operations Centre.

talentScarcity.note: Heavy equipment engineering, autonomous systems, and ~160 dealer service networks with decades of technician training pipelines. Hard to replicate but augmentable by AI over time.

bundling.note: Equipment + parts + service + Cat Financial + digital fleet management + autonomous technology — the most comprehensive bundle in heavy equipment. Competitors (Komatsu, Volvo CE, Hitachi) address subsets.

proprietaryData.note: Telemetry from millions of connected machines and 800+ autonomous trucks (11B+ tonnes hauled) feeds algorithm improvement and predictive maintenance. Real and growing, but not yet the primary moat lever.

regulatoryLockIn.note: EPA emissions standards, mine safety regulations (MSHA), global homologation, and nuclear/defence certifications create multi-year new-entrant barriers. Autonomous mining systems require separate regulatory qualification per jurisdiction.

networkEffects.note: ~160 independent dealers with ~2,700 branches create a density flywheel — more machines in a region attract more dealer investment, attracting more buyers. Strongest in North America and major mining regions.

transactionEmbedding.note: Cat Financial finances ~30-40% of equipment sales. Parts contracts, multi-year service agreements, and 15-25 year equipment life cycles create deep recurring revenue embedding. Services now ~40% of total revenue.

systemOfRecord.note: N/A — equipment manufacturer, not a system of record.

tenMoats.verdict: Caterpillar is a net beneficiary of AI — data centre power demand (E&T segment) and autonomous mining (Resource Industries) are structural tailwinds. The strongest moats are physical: dealer network density, regulatory lock-in, and transaction embedding through Cat Financial and multi-decade service cycles. No moats are meaningfully at risk from AI; the gap between moat score and AI resilience score reflects that CAT's core franchise is physical infrastructure, not software — AI adds to it but doesn't define it.
